notes:
Nóng means hot — usually about weather, temperature, or something you touch.  

It describes weather (trời nóng), objects (nước nóng), food, or your body (người nóng). It comes after the noun in Vietnamese.  

It can also mean spicy (đồ ăn nóng) and sometimes angry or heated in emotions (tính nóng). Context tells you which one.  

Opposite: lạnh (cold).  

You’ll also see it in phrases like tin nóng = breaking / urgent news (not about temperature!).
exampleSentences: Hôm nay trời rất nóng nên em muốn uống nước mát.
Anh nhớ bật điều hòa khi phòng bắt đầu nóng lên nhé.
Chị không thích ăn món này vì nó quá nóng.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Today the weather is very hot so I want to drink something cool.
Remember to turn on the air conditioner when the room starts to get hot.
I don't like eating this dish because it is too hot.

translation.en: Today the weather is very hot.

definitions.en: Having a high temperature; not cool.
definitions.vi: Có nhiệt độ cao; không mát.
definitions.es: Tener una alta temperatura; no fresco.

example.es: Hoy el clima está muy caliente.
example.en: Today the weather is very hot.

translation.en: Today the weather is very hot.

notes_md:
**Nóng** means *hot* — usually about weather, temperature, or something you touch.  

It describes weather (trời nóng), objects (nước nóng), food, or your body (người nóng). It comes after the noun in Vietnamese.  

It can also mean *spicy* (đồ ăn nóng) and sometimes *angry* or *heated* in emotions (tính nóng). Context tells you which one.  

Opposite: **lạnh** (cold).  

You’ll also see it in phrases like **tin nóng** = breaking / urgent news (not about temperature!).
